fundamentals.name: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. provides life sciences solutions, analytical instruments, specialty diagnostics, and laboratory products and biopharma services in the North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and internationally. It operates through four segments: Life Sciences Solutions, Analytical Instruments, Specialty Diagnostics, and Laboratory Products and Biopharma Services. The Life Sciences Solutions segment includes reagents, instruments, and consumables for biological and medical research; discovery and production of drugs and vaccines; and diagnosis of infections and diseases. Its Analytical Instruments segment provides instruments, consumables, software, and services for pharmaceutical, biotechnology, academic, government, environmental, and other research and industrial markets, as well as clinical laboratories. The Specialty Diagnostics segment offers clinical diagnostics products, such as liquid ready-to-use and lyophilized immunodiagnostic reagent kits, calibrators, controls, protein detection assays, and instruments; immunodiagnostic offerings comprising developing, manufacturing, and marketing of complete blood-test systems for the clinical diagnosis and monitoring of allergy, asthma and autoimmune diseases; microbiology offerings, such as dehydrated and prepared culture media, collection and transport systems, instrumentation and consumables to detect pathogens in blood, diagnostic and rapid direct specimen tests, quality-control products, and associated products; transplant diagnostics products, including human leukocyte antigen typing and testing for the organ transplant market; and healthcare market channel offerings. Its Laboratory Products and Biopharma Services segment provides laboratory products, research and safety market channel, and pharma services and clinical research. Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. was founded in 1956 and is headquartered in Waltham, Massachusetts.

judgment.summary: A large-cap scientific instruments and services company with solid growth, healthy margins, and a strong analyst consensus, though carrying significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 3.8%; next earnings date is 2026-07-22.
positives: Revenue growth 6.2% YoY and earnings growth 11.3% YoY indicate a healthy top and bottom line.
Profit margin of 15.2% and operating margin of 17.9% are robust.
Free cash flow of $5.52B TTM provides strong operational liquidity.
Forward P/E of 18.04x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 27.11x and analyst growth estimates.
25 analysts have a strong buy consensus with a mean target of $611.56.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 82.90% and total debt of $43.16B are substantial.
Quick ratio of 0.97 suggests potential short-term liquidity constraints.
PEG ratio of 1.62 indicates the stock may be somewhat richly valued relative to its earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) experienced a significant surge of 6.8% in its stock price recently, following its Analyst/Investor Day presentations and the announcement of its annual shareholder meeting results. While the stock has seen a notable upward move, some analysis suggests it may still be underperforming the broader S&P 500. The company's last reported quarter showed an earnings beat, and analysts maintain a strong buy consensus with a high average target price.
